# task\-brief\-git\-claim\-resolution — Resolve task briefs from current branch or worktree claim
status: done  priority: 2  scope: scope-git

Implement the spec §10 brief resolution step that uses current branch/worktree context when \-\-task is omitted\. If the current branch or worktree maps to exactly one active claim, Waystation can build the matching task brief automatically; ambiguous or missing mappings should return a short disambiguation result\.

## Acceptance
- brief can resolve the target task from current branch/worktree when \-\-task is omitted and exactly one active claim matches\.
- Ambiguous matches produce a coded diagnostic with candidate task ids instead of choosing silently\.
- No\-match cases keep a helpful error that tells the user to pass \-\-task\.
- The rendered brief includes branch/worktree context when present\.
- Tests cover exact match, ambiguous match, no match, and the existing \-\-task path\.
- bun test, typecheck, biome check, and waystation validate are clean\.
